title: Believability-Weighted Decisions

content.statement: Weight opinions in group decisions by track record and relevant expertise.
content.question: On this decision, whose opinion deserves the most weight based on a track record you can verify?
content.mental_model: Jury where votes count by qualification.
content.business_lens: In tough calls, score people’s past accuracy on similar topics; weight their input accordingly.
content.life_lens: Listen most carefully to people whose track record on the question at hand is strong.
sop: Define believability criteria per topic (track record, domain expertise)
If weighted consensus splits, escalate to a designated decider
content.explainer: Bridgewater’s Dot Collector lets people rate each other in real time. The weighted opinion guides the call. The discipline removes ego from disagreement: even Dalio defers when the weighted answer disagrees with him. It produces an idea meritocracy if applied honestly.
questions: On this decision, whose opinion deserves the most weight based on a track record you can verify?
Who in the room has the loudest opinion but the weakest track record on this specific topic?
Have you ever let seniority substitute for actual proven accuracy on a decision like this?

definitions.def: Giving more influence in a decision to people with a proven, verifiable track record on that specific topic, rather than weighting everyone's opinion equally.

content.equation_example: Score each person's opinion 1-10 for strength, multiplied by their Believability Weight, a 0.1 to 1.0 score based on track record. Person A: opinion 8 times weight 0.9 equals 7.2. Person B: opinion 9 times weight 0.3 equals 2.7. A higher total means the group's most-informed voices are steering the call. A lower total, or one unweighted loud voice dominating, means you're one bad opinion away from a costly mistake.
content.closing_statement: Weight opinions by track record, not volume.

caption:
Principle Nº 279: Believability-Weighted Decisions

Weight opinions in group decisions by track record and relevant expertise. Bridgewater’s Dot Collector lets people rate each other in real time.

Ask yourself: On this decision, whose opinion deserves the most weight based on a track record you can verify?
